scope: A SwornAgent operator can leave the TUI open and receive serial, non-overlapping catalog refreshes that atomically advance the releases pane and selected board from one shared snapshot while preserving identity selection, retaining the last good state on failure, and rendering recovery errors in every root view.

test_results.output_ref: live completion run 2026-07-18 — all three packages passed

live completion run 2026-07-18 — 4/4 acceptance checks covered

reachability.evidence: The root Model.Init/Update/View tests drive startup scheduling, due/result delivery, atomic list-plus-board replacement, alternate root views, recovery, and independent live/log ticks. Deterministic visible frames are committed at docs/release/2026-07-17-ref-aware-board-discovery/screenshots/S03-tui-live-board-refresh/{before,after,error}.txt and are asserted byte-for-byte by TestCatalogRefreshRenderedFrames.

delivered.item: AC-01 — Model.Init arms a serial background chain; one accepted CatalogRecord aggregate atomically replaces both releases and the selected board, renders S02-new, preserves merge/gate presentation, and re-arms only after completion without a secondary resolver read.

AC-02 — in-flight state prevents overlap, monotonically comparable generations reject stale delivery without visible mutation, refresh messages remain distinct, and live/log tick chains retain their single-chain behavior.

AC-03 — release and slice cursors follow stable IDs across insertion/reorder and clamp deterministically when identities disappear; removing the selected board clears it and returns a board-visible operator to releases without panic.

AC-04 — discovery failure retains the complete last-good snapshot, renders refresh errors alongside unrelated errors in releases, board, live, log, blocked, and settings, re-arms, and clears only the refresh error on recovery.

divergence.description: Captain PIN-1 authorised and required adding internal/tui/board.go so pure catalog-only board hydration could be separated from startup-only ActiveMerges decoration; the touchpoint was declared in status.json before editing.
Captain PIN-4 added committed deterministic terminal frames under the release screenshots directory as visible reachability evidence.
